VI. ERPATISTAN AND n!raNGISTAn. 327 39. If the Zaotar be in a state of sin and the assistants be righteous, If they know that he is in a state of sin, What they recite themselves is accepted.
If they know not that he is in a state of sin», the whole of the G&thas is accepted.
If the Zaotar be righteous and the assistants be righteous, the whole is accepted.
If the Zaotar be in a state of sin and the assistants be also in a state of sin, neither the one nor the other is accepted.
40'. Any one of the faithful is accepted as a Zaotar, Even a woman * or a child, If he know the ends and the heads of the chapters*, And know how to perform the acts of ritual between the chapters.
NIrangistAn.

41. He who does not sing the Githas, either out of unbelief, or out of impiety, becomes a Pesh6tanu. What is unbelief* ? What is impiety * ? It is renouncing the Religion of Mazda. 42*. He who stays the year through without singing the Githas becomes a Pesh6tanu.
